BEACH.label: At the beach
BEACH.intro: Enjoy the sea — leave only footprints! This checklist keeps your beach day low-carbon.

items.label: Bring a reusable bag & water bottle
items.tip: Avoids ~30g plastic + bottle waste

items.label: Say no to plastic straws & cutlery
items.tip: One straw ≈ 1g plastic that can reach the ocean

items.label: Carry your trash back — don't burn or bury
items.tip: Burning plastic releases toxic CO₂ and fumes

items.label: Came by bus, train, or shared ride?
items.tip: One car trip can be 5× more CO₂ than public transport

items.label: Use reef-safe sunscreen (no micro-plastics)
items.tip: Microbeads pollute water and harm fish

items.label: Don't feed animals packaged snacks
items.tip: Wrapper litter is a top beach pollution source

SCHOOL.label: At school / college
SCHOOL.intro: Small habits at school add up! Tick what you did today.

items.label: Brought reusable water bottle
items.tip: 1 less PET bottle ≈ 25g plastic + 0.15 kg CO₂

items.label: Homemade lunch in a tiffin box
items.tip: Skips delivery packaging & fuel for one order

items.label: Took bus, metro, or walked
items.tip: School commute by car daily ≈ 3 kg CO₂/week

items.label: Switched off fans & lights when leaving class
items.tip: 1 hour of 2 fans ≈ 0.15 kWh

items.label: Used both sides of notebook paper
items.tip: Less paper = less tree cutting & processing energy

items.label: Put paper/plastic in separate bins
items.tip: Recycling 100g plastic saves landfill methane

HOME.label: At home
HOME.intro: Home is where big savings happen — electricity, cooking, and waste.

items.label: Unplugged chargers & TV when not in use
items.tip: Standby power can be 5–10% of home electricity

items.label: AC at 26°C or used fan instead
items.tip: Each 1°C lower on AC ≈ 6% more power

items.label: Used LED bulbs in main rooms
items.tip: LED uses 75% less energy than old bulbs

items.label: Segregated wet & dry waste
items.tip: Less landfill = less methane (strong greenhouse gas)

items.label: Used solar or dried clothes in sun
items.tip: Dryer/skipping solar offset saves grid power

items.label: Avoided extra delivery app order
items.tip: One less delivery ≈ 0.08 kg CO₂ + 20g plastic

TRAVEL.label: Out travelling / trip
TRAVEL.intro: Travel days have high impact — these steps keep it lower.

items.label: Chose train, metro, or bus over taxi
items.tip: Train emits ~70% less CO₂ per km than solo car

items.label: Turned off AC & lights leaving hotel room
items.tip: Hotel energy is Scope 2 — still counts globally

items.label: Refilled bottle instead of buying new
items.tip: Avoids PET bottle production emissions

items.label: Ate local / carried snacks
items.tip: Fewer food-delivery trips while travelling

items.label: Skipped hotel plastic laundry bag
items.tip: Small plastics add up on trips

MARKET.label: Shopping / market
MARKET.intro: Smart shopping cuts plastic and food miles.

items.label: Brought cloth or jute bag
items.tip: One LDPE bag ≈ 10–20g plastic

items.label: Bought local seasonal produce
items.tip: Less transport & cold storage energy

items.label: Bought larger pack instead of many small ones
items.tip: Less packaging per gram of product

items.label: Skipped impulse packaged snacks
items.tip: Multilayer chips packs are hard to recycle
